what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment identifier to a target image URI and a creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-assets/icons/image (29)
No body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: image:// URI target referencing a numbered icon asset
attachment record is channel-scoped under design-assets
conventions: Uses a channel-memory attachment file naming pattern att-<uuid>.md
concerns: Target URI contains a space and parentheses which may need encoding in some consumers
No checksum, size, or uploader fields to verify or audit the attachment
notes: This is a lightweight channel attachment ledger entry that points to an image asset but carries minimal provenance metadata.
questions: What does 'image (29)' refer to — a filename, a generated thumbnail, or an indexed asset?
Is the target URI resolvable by the consuming system?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: It appears to serve as an attachment index entry; the content matches that purpose, though it lacks descriptive or provenance details often expected in an attachment record.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to catalog an image attachment within a design-assets channel so that the asset can be located and associated with channel activity.
novelty: Use of an image:// scheme with a human-readable, space-containing path rather than a conventional file or HTTP URL.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment identifier and a pointer to a channel-specific image asset, which a fused system can use to correlate attachments with channel content.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that records an image attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (29) with a creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To index and locate an image asset attached to the design-assets channel.
